title: Alert Storm Detection (Alertmanager fanout, PagerDuty and Opsgenie bursts, Splunk episode floods, Datadog, New Relic, Observability Cloud spikes, deduplicated fingerprints versus duplicate_ratio, change-window correlation)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.3.2 Prometheus metric ingest health analytics, per-monitor SLO burn calculators in isolation, or secure software supply chain attestations. Does not auto-suppress production alerts without human-approved playbooks; it produces evidence and routing guidance for governed noise-scrub workflows.
evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_3_5_alert_storm_noise_scrub on a five-minute schedule; exported CSV joined to oncall_routing.csv; ticketed timelines from PagerDuty and Alertmanager silence logs for the same service_id.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, replay fifty duplicate Alertmanager webhooks within one minute while PagerDuty mirrors the same dedup key, confirm observability_uc_13_3_5_alert_storm_noise_scrub returns critical or high with elevated duplicate_ratio and non-null recommended_action within two five-minute windows.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After clearing test traffic and restoring normal routing, when baselines fall and change_calendar shows no overlapping window, filtering the saved search to critical and high should return zero rows for thirty consecutive runs.

dataSources: `index=observability` (or `alerts_ingest`) with `sourcetype=prometheus:alertmanager:webhook` for Alertmanager notification payloads including fingerprint and label tuples, `sourcetype=pagerduty:incident:v3` or `sourcetype=pagerduty:webhook` for PagerDuty incidents and dedup keys, `sourcetype=opsgenie:alerts` for Opsgenie alert API or webhook mirrors, `index=itsi_grouped_alerts` with `sourcetype=itsi:episode` or `sourcetype=itsi_grouped_alerts` for episode metadata, `sourcetype=datadog:monitor`, `sourcetype=newrelic:incident`, or `sourcetype=signalfx:detector` for vendor detector lanes, `index=cmdb` `sourcetype=change_calendar` for maintenance and deploy windows, lookup `oncall_routing.csv` keyed on `service_id` for escalation_policy and rotation_name.
app: Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud plus HTTP Event Collector or TA-fed observability indexes; optional Splunk ITSI and Splunk SOAR for episode and playbook automation
spl:
`comment("UC-13.3.5 alert storm correlation multisearch AlertManager PagerDuty Opsgenie ITSI vendor APM earliest=-2h@h coalesce enrich")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=prometheus:alertmanager:webhook earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval source_system="alertmanager"
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service, labels_service, namespace, job, "unk_svc"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(alertname, labels_alertname, "prom_alert")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dc, region, labels_region, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(fingerprint, labels_fingerprint, strcat(alertname,"|",instance), md5(_raw))))
      | eval is_escalation=if(match(lower(coalesce(status,"")),"firing"),1,0)
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
    [ search index=observability (sourcetype=pagerduty:incident:v3 OR sourcetype=pagerduty:webhook) earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval source_system="pagerduty"
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_id, service, "pd_unk"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(summary, title, description, "pd_incident")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, datacenter, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(incident_key, dedup_key, id, strcat(service_id,summary))))
      | eval is_escalation=1
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=opsgenie:alerts earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval source_system="opsgenie"
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(team_id, alias, entity, "og_unk"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(message, alias, "og_alert")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(tinyId, alias, strcat(message,entity))))
      | eval is_escalation=if(match(priority,"P1|P2"),1,0)
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-2h@h latest=now (sourcetype=itsi:episode OR sourcetype=itsi_grouped_alerts OR source=*episode*)
      | eval source_system="itsi_episode"
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_id, itsi_service_id, "itsi_unk"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(title, rule_name, "itsi_episode")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(episode_id, itsi_group_id, rule_id, md5(_raw))))
      | eval is_escalation=1
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
    [ search index=observability (sourcetype=datadog:monitor OR sourcetype=newrelic:incident OR sourcetype=signalfx:detector) earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval source_system=case(sourcetype=="datadog:monitor","datadog", sourcetype=="newrelic:incident","newrelic", true(),"splunk_observability")
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service, host, monitor_id, detector_id, "vendor_unk"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(monitor_name, policy_name, detector_name, "vendor_alert")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(event_id, monitor_id, incident_id, strcat(service_id,monitor_name))))
      | eval is_escalation=1
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
| eval service_id=coalesce(service_id,"unknown")
| eval service_name=coalesce(service_name,"unnamed")
| eval region=coalesce(region,"global")
| eval duplicate_count=alert_count-dedup_alert_count
| eval duplicate_ratio=if(alert_count>0, round(duplicate_count*100/alert_count,4), 0)
| eventstats median(alert_count) AS baseline_min_rate BY service_id
| eventstats dc(service_id) AS unique_services_count BY _time
| sort 0 + service_id + _time + source_system
| streamstats window=3 global=f current=t sum(alert_count) AS storm_rate_3m BY service_id source_system
| eval storm_rate_1m=coalesce(alert_count,0)
| eval severity=case(
    storm_rate_1m>=50 OR (storm_rate_1m>=3*coalesce(baseline_min_rate,1) AND unique_services_count>=10), "critical",
    storm_rate_1m>=25 OR (storm_rate_1m>=2*coalesce(baseline_min_rate,1) AND unique_services_count>=6), "high",
    storm_rate_1m>=12 OR storm_rate_1m>=1.5*coalesce(baseline_min_rate,1), "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "invoke_noise_scrub_playbook_page_incident_commander_review_am_inhibits_pd_service_suppress",
    severity=="high", "route_reliability_chat_throttle_fanout_audit_group_interval_dedup_keys",
    severity=="medium", "notify_platform_ops_tune_opsgenie_policies_itsi_neap_windows",
    true(), "log_only_continue_baseline_calibration")
| join type=left max=0 service_id
    [| inputlookup oncall_routing.csv
     | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,svc_id,""))))
     | fields service_id escalation_policy rotation_name]
| join type=left max=0 service_id
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=change_calendar earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(service_id)))
      | stats latest(window_label) AS recent_change_window BY service_id ]
| eval escalation_policy=coalesce(escalation_policy,"unset")
| eval rotation_name=coalesce(rotation_name,"unset")
| eval recent_change_window=coalesce(recent_change_window,"none_noted")
| table _time, source_system, service_id, service_name, region, alert_count, dedup_alert_count, duplicate_ratio, escalation_count, unique_services_count, recent_change_window, severity, recommended_action
description: Detects alert storms across observability and ticketing pipelines by correlating one-minute alert volume with deduplicated fingerprints across Alertmanager fanout, PagerDuty incidents, Opsgenie streams, Splunk episode queues, and Datadog, New Relic, or Splunk Observability Cloud spikes, then enriches rows with on-call routing and recent change windows to steer responders toward suppression and grouping fixes before paging fatigue sets in.
value: Reliability leaders gain an early warning when notification systems overload on-call rotations during single-root-cause outages, hybrid migration cutovers, or policy misconfigurations. Executives receive defensible evidence that distinguishes repetitive webhook noise from genuine multi-service blast radius, preserving trust in escalation paths and reducing mean time to remediate routing mistakes.
implementation: Ingest Alertmanager webhooks, PagerDuty incidents, Opsgenie alerts, episode metadata, and vendor monitor events into observability indexes with normalized service_id; maintain oncall_routing.csv and cmdb change_calendar; save observability_uc_13_3_5_alert_storm_noise_scrub on a five-minute cadence with earliest=-2h@h; route critical and high severities to the reliability queue with noise-scrub playbook links; archive weekly CSV exports to your governance index.
visualization: Severity-colored table by service_id and source_system with duplicate_ratio sparklines, drilldowns to raw webhook JSON, PagerDuty timelines, Opsgenie alert detail, episode review links, and change_calendar overlays; single-value tiles for unique_services_count per minute.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Alerts.signature) AS sig latest(Alerts.severity) AS sev count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
| rename Alerts.dest AS alert_host
| join type=left max=0 alert_host [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Change.object) AS chg latest(Change.action) AS act FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.dest
| rename Change.dest AS alert_host ]
| join type=left max=0 alert_host [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS alert_host ]
| table alert_host sig sev chg act cpu mem
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Alerts summaries for correlation-friendly dest and signature fields when alert events are CIM-mapped; accelerate Change for cmdb-aligned change objects tied to service_id; accelerate Performance when underlying hosts map to alert-producing services; retain thirty days for quarterly reliability attestations.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Review episodes in ITSI
Google SRE — Workbook: Alerting on SLOs
Google SRE Book — Monitoring distributed systems
Splunk Docs — Use the Common Information Model Add-on

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate major incidents naturally spike alert_count across many services; mitigate by confirming duplicate_ratio and correlating with customer-facing error budgets before dismissing the storm as noise. Planned change windows may intentionally raise alert volume during canaries; require change_calendar tags and temporary threshold offsets rather than permanent mutes. Intentional load and chaos tests can fire synthetic monitors; gate test environments or label events with drill metadata so severity downgrades automatically. Alert policy onboarding bursts when teams import dozens of monitors at once; schedule imports and use warn-tier routing until baselines stabilize. Observability vendor retries during partial API outages can inflate duplicate_ratio with identical fingerprints; coordinate with vendor status pages and backoff configuration. Episode maintenance during content pack upgrades can pause grouping while vendor lanes remain hot; avoid blaming infrastructure until NEAP policies finish rebalancing.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode index lanes and NEAP correlation when episode objects feed the same observability indexes

premiumApps.name: Splunk SOAR
premiumApps.note: optional — noise-scrub and paging governance playbooks triggered from critical severity rows

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with site reliability leadership, observability engineers who curate Prometheus and Alertmanager routing, incident commanders who administer PagerDuty and Opsgenie policies, and the operators who maintain Splunk correlation indexes plus optional automation playbooks. This use case is deliberately about human paging economics: when dozens of tools all scream at once, the organization loses the ability to see a single causal thread, rotations burn out, and executive trust in alerting collapses even if the underlying outage is straightforward. The detection therefore fuses raw alert volume per minute with deduplicated uniqueness so flapping duplicates cannot masquerade as a multi-service catastrophe, then overlays maintenance metadata and recent change labels before anyone escalates a noise-scrub workflow.

Alertmanager fans out notifications after Prometheus rule evaluation. Inhibition rules, silences, and route matchers decide which receivers see which subset of group labels. Misconfigured inhibition can hide legitimate pages, while absent inhibition during dependency failures produces identical alerts for every leaf pod. PagerDuty and Opsgenie translate those notifications into incidents, escalations, and on-call rotations; each vendor encodes deduplication keys, urgency, and service ownership differently. Splunk episode analytics groups related notables into episode objects with policies that can throttle, merge, or split streams; when aggregation policies drift, episode queues flood even while KPI health scores look stable. Datadog, New Relic, and Splunk Observability Cloud emit their own monitor and detector events, often landing in parallel indexes during hybrid observability migrations.

RBAC and escalation matrices matter as much as signal processing. Read-only automation accounts should pull webhook payloads, REST incident exports, and configuration backups without standing privilege on production namespaces. Store API tokens in Splunk credential storage or an external vault with rotation contracts. Document which roles may acknowledge storms, who may attach suppressions, and who may edit routing rules during an incident; otherwise a well-meaning engineer silences the wrong subtree while finance-critical workloads remain unguarded. Legal reviewers should confirm customer identifiers are stripped from alert labels before indexing when regulations require minimization.

Risk briefing: counting alerts without deduplication punishes teams that emit high-cardinality labels or replay webhooks during retries. This control keeps duplicate_ratio visible so responders distinguish repetitive chirps from genuinely broad failures. Baselines use eventstats medians per service_id over the search window; shorten or lengthen dispatch.earliest_time to match your organization's seasonality. Correlation with change_calendar does not prove causation; it prioritizes interviews with change owners before blame spreads.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.3.2 focuses on Prometheus metric ingest plumbing health, scrape targets, and collector queues rather than paging fanout economics. UC-13.3.5 centers on alert storms across notification paths, deduplicated fingerprints, escalation density, and operational routing into noise-scrub playbooks before pagers melt.

Anti-patterns to retire early include per-host alerts for stateless fleets where service-level burn would suffice, dashboards that page on chart thresholds without routing discipline, and Alertmanager group_interval values so short that the same group re-notifies every evaluation tick. Prefer service-oriented SLO burn alerts with explicit multi-window policies, then let dedup keys remain stable across deploys.

Splunk SOAR can subscribe to the same index stream: trigger a playbook when severity is critical and recent_change_window is none_noted, requiring human approval before auto-acknowledging vendor incidents. Keep playbook concurrency limits conservative so a genuine storm does not spawn hundreds of parallel remediations.

Escalation matrix RBAC should map service_id tiers to Slack bridges, executive bridges, and break-glass phone trees. Test quarterly that low-tier services cannot hijack executive bridges during storms.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands Alertmanager webhook JSON or normalized rows with sourcetype=prometheus:alertmanager:webhook inside an observability or alerts_ingest index. Preserve labels that identify service, namespace, cluster, region, alertname, instance, and fingerprint. If your forwarder truncates wide JSON, raise TRUNCATE and validate fingerprints remain intact or derive stable md5 anchors from canonical label tuples.

Lane B ingests PagerDuty v3 incidents or webhook mirrors as sourcetype=pagerduty:incident:v3 or sourcetype=pagerduty:webhook. Map service identifiers, escalation_policy ids, dedup keys, and urgency. Align time zones so Splunk _time reflects incident creation, not just log shipment.

Lane C captures Opsgenie Alerts API pull or webhook posts as sourcetype=opsgenie:alerts with team, alias, priority, and entity fields. Normalize multiline messages into single-line summaries for stats commands.

Lane D reads Splunk episode metadata from index=itsi_grouped_alerts with sourcetype=itsi:episode or sourcetype=itsi_grouped_alerts depending on your deployment. Include episode_id, service_id, severity, and rule identifiers. If episodes are mirrored elsewhere, duplicate only after confirming field parity.

Lane E collects vendor APM or observability alerts such as sourcetype=datadog:monitor, sourcetype=newrelic:incident, or sourcetype=signalfx:detector into the same observability index family so multisearch arms coalesce without brittle index=* patterns in production.

Maintain oncall_routing.csv with columns service_id, escalation_policy, rotation_name, tier, and optional region overrides. Refresh from PagerDuty service directories or your service catalog nightly. Maintain cmdb change_calendar with sourcetype=change_calendar, fields service_id, window_label, deploy_id, and change_ticket correlation.

props.conf should extract fingerprint, status, and labels from Alertmanager payloads using KV_MODE=json when appropriate. For PagerDuty, use indexed fields for dedup keys to accelerate joins. Test each lane with a one-hour window before enabling the scheduled search.

Example curl helpers for design reviews from a bastion with credentials:

```bash
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Token token=REDACTED" https://api.pagerduty.com/incidents | head
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: GenieKey REDACTED" https://api.opsgenie.com/v2/alerts | head
```

Volume controls: route high-volume test stacks to a sandbox index excluded from production thresholds, or tag events with environment=lab so eval filters drop them.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_3_5_alert_storm_noise_scrub with a five-minute schedule during steady operations, dispatch.earliest_time=-2h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate service_id and source_system pairs for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, duplicate_ratio, and recent_change_window in pager narratives so incident commanders open inhibition runbooks before muting blindly.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Alertmanager, PagerDuty, Opsgenie, ITSI episode, and vendor APM arms so a silent lane in one vendor cannot hide a cross-tool storm. Per-arm one-minute bucketing yields alert_count and dedup_alert_count before union. eventstats median builds a service baseline; eventstats dc counts how many distinct services fire in the same minute to catch wide blast radius. streamstats sums a short rolling window for smoothing flaps. case() encodes severity using both absolute rates and baseline multiples. coalesce() backfills missing service_id, region, and routing metadata after lookup joins. The first join attaches on-call routing; the second join attaches change calendar context.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.5 alert storm correlation multisearch AlertManager PagerDuty Opsgenie ITSI vendor APM earliest=-2h@h coalesce enrich")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=prometheus:alertmanager:webhook earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval source_system="alertmanager"
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service, labels_service, namespace, job, "unk_svc"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(alertname, labels_alertname, "prom_alert")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(dc, region, labels_region, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(fingerprint, labels_fingerprint, strcat(alertname,"|",instance), md5(_raw))))
      | eval is_escalation=if(match(lower(coalesce(status,"")),"firing"),1,0)
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
    [ search index=observability (sourcetype=pagerduty:incident:v3 OR sourcetype=pagerduty:webhook) earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval source_system="pagerduty"
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_id, service, "pd_unk"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(summary, title, description, "pd_incident")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, datacenter, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(incident_key, dedup_key, id, strcat(service_id,summary))))
      | eval is_escalation=1
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
    [ search index=observability sourcetype=opsgenie:alerts earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval source_system="opsgenie"
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(team_id, alias, entity, "og_unk"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(message, alias, "og_alert")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(tinyId, alias, strcat(message,entity))))
      | eval is_escalation=if(match(priority,"P1|P2"),1,0)
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-2h@h latest=now (sourcetype=itsi:episode OR sourcetype=itsi_grouped_alerts OR source=*episode*)
      | eval source_system="itsi_episode"
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_id, itsi_service_id, "itsi_unk"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(title, rule_name, "itsi_episode")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(episode_id, itsi_group_id, rule_id, md5(_raw))))
      | eval is_escalation=1
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
    [ search index=observability (sourcetype=datadog:monitor OR sourcetype=newrelic:incident OR sourcetype=signalfx:detector) earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval source_system=case(sourcetype=="datadog:monitor","datadog", sourcetype=="newrelic:incident","newrelic", true(),"splunk_observability")
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service, host, monitor_id, detector_id, "vendor_unk"))))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(monitor_name, policy_name, detector_name, "vendor_alert")))
      | eval region=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(region, "global"))))
      | eval fingerprint=trim(toString(coalesce(event_id, monitor_id, incident_id, strcat(service_id,monitor_name))))
      | eval is_escalation=1
      | bucket _time span=1m
      | stats count AS rawc dc(fingerprint) AS ddfp sum(is_escalation) AS esc BY _time source_system service_id service_name region
      | eval alert_count=rawc
      | eval dedup_alert_count=ddfp
      | eval escalation_count=esc
      | fields _time source_system service_id service_name region alert_count dedup_alert_count escalation_count ]
| eval service_id=coalesce(service_id,"unknown")
| eval service_name=coalesce(service_name,"unnamed")
| eval region=coalesce(region,"global")
| eval duplicate_count=alert_count-dedup_alert_count
| eval duplicate_ratio=if(alert_count>0, round(duplicate_count*100/alert_count,4), 0)
| eventstats median(alert_count) AS baseline_min_rate BY service_id
| eventstats dc(service_id) AS unique_services_count BY _time
| sort 0 + service_id + _time + source_system
| streamstats window=3 global=f current=t sum(alert_count) AS storm_rate_3m BY service_id source_system
| eval storm_rate_1m=coalesce(alert_count,0)
| eval severity=case(
    storm_rate_1m>=50 OR (storm_rate_1m>=3*coalesce(baseline_min_rate,1) AND unique_services_count>=10), "critical",
    storm_rate_1m>=25 OR (storm_rate_1m>=2*coalesce(baseline_min_rate,1) AND unique_services_count>=6), "high",
    storm_rate_1m>=12 OR storm_rate_1m>=1.5*coalesce(baseline_min_rate,1), "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "invoke_noise_scrub_playbook_page_incident_commander_review_am_inhibits_pd_service_suppress",
    severity=="high", "route_reliability_chat_throttle_fanout_audit_group_interval_dedup_keys",
    severity=="medium", "notify_platform_ops_tune_opsgenie_policies_itsi_neap_windows",
    true(), "log_only_continue_baseline_calibration")
| join type=left max=0 service_id
    [| inputlookup oncall_routing.csv
     | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,svc_id,""))))
     | fields service_id escalation_policy rotation_name]
| join type=left max=0 service_id
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=change_calendar earliest=-2h@h latest=now
      | eval service_id=lower(trim(toString(service_id)))
      | stats latest(window_label) AS recent_change_window BY service_id ]
| eval escalation_policy=coalesce(escalation_policy,"unset")
| eval rotation_name=coalesce(rotation_name,"unset")
| eval recent_change_window=coalesce(recent_change_window,"none_noted")
| table _time, source_system, service_id, service_name, region, alert_count, dedup_alert_count, duplicate_ratio, escalation_count, unique_services_count, recent_change_window, severity, recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_3_5_alert_storm_noise_scrub_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -2h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-sre-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Alert storm $result.severity$ $result.service_id$ dup=$result.duplicate_ratio$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_3_5_alert_storm_noise_scrub | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal SLOs, summarize each arm into a one-minute summary index keyed on service_id and source_system, widen alert searches to earliest=-90m on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Alertmanager UI: compare active silences and inhibition graphs to Splunk rows for the same minute. If Splunk shows a storm but Alertmanager shows a single group, verify webhook fanout duplicates or multiple receivers.

PagerDuty validation: open the service incident timeline and compare dedup keys to fingerprint fields in Splunk. Opsgenie validation: confirm alert small ids align with tinyId extractions.

ITSI validation: open Episode Review and compare episode counts per service_id to itsi_episode rows. If episodes look quiet while vendor lanes scream, NEAP scope may exclude those notables.

Change correlation: pick a row with recent_change_window populated and confirm the change ticket matches service_id owners.

Synthetic drill: inject a controlled burst of duplicate webhooks in non-production and confirm duplicate_ratio rises while dedup_alert_count stays bounded.

Negative test: after suppressing test traffic, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for thirty consecutive runs.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Cascading dependency failure such as internal DNS flaps: every leaf service pages simultaneously; use inhibition templates that silence leaf alerts while a single resolver alert pages, then validate duplicate_ratio drops once grouping keys align.

Case 2 — Expired TLS certificate wave across an ingress class: dedup_alert_count stays low because fingerprints cluster on the same cert name; escalate through certificate operations, attach change_calendar entries for renewal trains.

Case 3 — cert-manager controller bug replays certificate not-ready events: storm_rate_1m oscillates; compare streamstats windows to controller logs, patch or roll back the controller before muting.

Case 4 — Region failover migration duplicates monitors in both regions: unique_services_count inflates; validate monitor ownership tags and temporary dual-write policies, downgrade severity only with executive approval.

Case 5 — Runaway flapping deployment retries: duplicate_ratio approaches one hundred with tiny dedup_alert_count; route to release engineering, freeze pipeline, and review health check thresholds.

Case 6 — Container churn from misconfigured HPA: per-pod alerts storm; shift alerting to workload-level signals and fix HPA targets before reopening paging.

Case 7 — AlertManager group_interval misconfiguration causing repeat notifications: audit route trees and group_wait versus group_interval, align with SRE workbook guidance on alert spacing.

Case 8 — Deduplication key drift after label renames: PagerDuty incidents fragment; rebuild dedup templates and service_id joins in oncall_routing.csv before blaming infrastructure.

Case 9 — Episode policy aggregation breakdown after content pack upgrade: NEAP splits episodes excessively; replay policy diffs, restore correlation rules, and validate itsi_grouped_alerts counts stabilize.

Case 10 — On-call routing loop where two services escalate each other: inspect escalation_policy in lookup joins, break circular schedules, and document ownership in the escalation matrix.

Case 11 — Dashboards-as-alerts antipattern noise where visual thresholds page continuously: move thresholds into proper monitors with dedup and silence discipline, archive dashboard-only alerts.

Case 12 — Vendor retry storms during partial API outages: duplicate_ratio high with repeating fingerprints; negotiate vendor backoff settings and temporary service suppressions with incident commander approval.
grandmaExplanation: Imagine a concert hall where every exit sign has its own siren on one flaky switch. When the switch twitches, identical screams fill the room and nobody hears the usher who knows which door is safe. We tally sirens per minute, separate real distinct alarms from echoes, and read posted notices before waking the neighborhood.
hardware: Applies to cloud-hosted observability stacks, Kubernetes-hosted Alertmanager and Prometheus, SaaS incident providers, and Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tiers indexing webhook and API mirrors.
